author: Sofia Martínez
title: Roofing Companies: Stop Relying on Yellow Pages for New Leads
description: Local SEO, Google Ads, and review strategy for roofing contractors. Proven tactics that generate 8-15 leads per month without the middleman.

sections.p: Roofing is a local business, and local businesses are in the middle of a digital transformation that most haven't noticed yet. Homeowners aren't calling Yellow Pages or asking neighbors—they're searching 'roof repair near me' or 'roofer [city name]' and clicking the first 3 results on Google. If you're not in those 3 results, you're invisible. We've worked with 18 roofing companies in the past 2 years, and the best-performing ones get 40-60% of their leads from Google (organic + Ads), not referrals or legacy lead services.

sections.h2: Local SEO for Roofing: The Foundation

sections.p: Your Google Business Profile is your most important asset. Not your website. Not your reviews (though those matter). Your GBP. If it's not optimized, you're losing 30-40% of potential leads to competitors who did basic work. Start here: photos. Upload 15-20 professional photos of roofs you've repaired, your team, your vehicles, your office. Use your phone if you have to, but make them clear. We audited a roofer in Nashville who had 2 photos on their GBP. They added 18 new photos and their 'view business' clicks increased by 57% in 8 weeks.

list: Upload 15+ high-quality photos: roof work in progress, finished installations, team members, vehicles with branding, office
Write a detailed service description (150-200 words) that includes specific services: 'residential roof repair,' 'metal roofing,' 'emergency roof leaks,' with your city name mentioned 2-3 times
Add business hours, service areas (list 5-8 neighborhoods or cities if you're regional), and a clear call-to-action button (call or website)
Ask your 5 best customers to leave reviews on your GBP; aim for 15-20 reviews minimum, refresh them quarterly

sections.h2: Google Ads: Predictable Leads You Control

sections.p: Google Ads for local services is the fastest way to generate leads while you build organic rankings. A roofing company in Denver we worked with launched a Google Ads campaign targeting keywords like 'roof repair Denver,' 'emergency roofer,' 'roof leak repair.' First month: $1,200 spend, 12 clicks, 3 qualified leads. Second month (after optimization): $1,200 spend, 28 clicks, 7 leads. Third month: $1,400 spend, 35 clicks, 9 leads. Cost per lead dropped from $400 to $155.

The key is not competing on high-volume keywords. Don't bid on 'roofing' or 'roof repair'—too expensive, too broad. Target specific problems and locations: 'emergency roof leak [city],' '[city] hail damage roof,' 'roof replacement [neighborhood],' 'metal roof installation [city].' These have lower search volume but higher intent and lower cost per click. Budget $800-1,500 per month and test for 6-8 weeks before scaling.

sections.quote: Your website doesn't need to rank #1 organically if you're in Google Ads top 3. You get the lead either way. The mistake is doing one or the other. Do both.

sections.h2: Your Website's One Job: Convert Calls and Quotes

sections.p: Your website doesn't need to be beautiful. It needs to convert scared homeowners into calls or quote requests. A typical roofing company website has 10+ pages of fluff. Cut it to 3: your main service page (before/after photos, what you fix, why you're trustworthy), your service area page (list cities and neighborhoods), and a simple quote/contact page with a phone number you actually answer and an email form.

list: Put your phone number in the header on every page in large text (people don't fill forms; they call)
Add a 'Schedule Free Inspection' call-to-action button above the fold on your home and services pages
Include before/after project photos on your services page (more effective than testimonials)
Add a local schema markup snippet so Google knows you're a roofing company in specific cities (we'll link a template)

sections.p: Reviews directly impact your Google ranking in your local area. A roofing company with 8 reviews will rank below a company with 25 reviews, even if both have similar SEO. Ask every customer for a review, but do it right: send a text message 2 days after they pay the final invoice with a link to your Google review (not Facebook, not Yelp—Google first). Make it stupid simple. 'Hey, we loved working on your roof! Would you mind leaving us a quick review here? [link]' Get 15% response rate minimum.

Expect to collect 3-5 new reviews per month if you have 8-12 jobs per month. That's 36-60 per year. In 18 months, you'll have 60+ reviews. That's a competitive moat. Companies with 60+ reviews at 4.7+ stars convert quote requests at 2-3x the rate of companies with 5 reviews.
